tokens: Okay; and standing by for a mark when you roll.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Hey, your camera's loose on the swivel, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, I'm getting a pan, here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, I just wanted to make sure it was running.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...course 346, and it's about 1.7 clicks to Station 4.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'm going to go down sort of slow here, Joe.
Just to make sure we play it cool.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: When you finish photography, we're standing by for a description.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, I just had the camera running, Joe.
Remind me to turn it off when it runs out of film.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We've got about half a mag on it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And you're running at 12 frames per second, I imagine.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I think I know what that is.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Down-Sun isn't going to be very good on the photography, Joe, because the zero phase just washes out completely.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Jim might want to swing the camera around and point it more towards the right.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, we're heading directly downhill, now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I'm looking out at the...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, are we looking at the big crater dead ahead?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: But, again, that's...You know, we didn't see the levee, or rampart, on the eastern side.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: So probably any place on the southern rim would be good.
(Pause) Although, from here, it almost looks like you could drive around the eastern rim of Dune.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Boy, there's a crater just east of - east of Dune; it looks very recent, and it has a great number of blocks that I can see from here.
And the largest from this vantage point...again, y'all have probably seen it on TV.
The largest crater, which we named
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Lost under Joe) our tracks here, as we go downslope.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Probably just follow the tracks, huh?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We know that's a fairly good route.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; we're heading 320; bearing's 350, and range is 4.3.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It's bouncier going down, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, we're about down out of it, now.
Well, we didn't get the 500 in stereo up there, but you got a pan, didn't you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, I can't get over those
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That's the most organized mountain I've seen.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Laughing) Yeah, they're so uniform in width.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Nothing we've seen before (during training) has had the same thickness of each bed.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Uniform thickness from the very top to the bottom.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And looking to the north on that spur that we talked about yesterday, we can see the horizontal bed again.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Any idea of the dimension on that thickness?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Actually, I'd estimate it's relatively thin.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I'd say that if you took the ridge line on Mount Hadley, which is practically horizontal at our present position, and split that into 100 percent, then I'd say those lineations across there, the bedding across there, are probably like a quarter of a percent.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: If you look across the ridge line and then look at the dip to the northwest there, you could count a couple of hundred, anyway; couldn't you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Apparently you couldn't see that on TV.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I wouldn't think they'd be able to make out detail like that.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hopefully, it's in the photographs, but we're marking it down...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And then, Jim, if you look horizontal, well, all the way up, I guess that...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It just looks like slump, probably.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Because they're discontinuous, subhorizontal lines, which are pretty much cross-bedded, if it was bedding, and I don't think it is.
It just looks like slump-pattern ground.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And what kind of progress are you making now, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, we're going at about point-8 clicks.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Laughing as he defends his honor) Oh!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) And we're going up a slight slope, following our track.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, here's a little tilt (probably sideways).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: There's the LM directly ahead of us.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Bearing is...Yeah, bearing is right on.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, that's quite a Nav system.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And are you progressing towards Dune Crater now?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We thought when we got up here just south of Dune, we'd probably head north-northeast.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...And just a factor into your thinking, we can afford a very short stop in the vicinity of Station 4.
It doesn't have to be really very close (to the planned stopping place).
We're interested in either documented samples or a rake sample there, if you think it looks like a good area for a rake sample.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: But you'd still like the station to be on the southern rim, I would think.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Why don't you head for right there, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...or Autolycus, Jim, but the exact position's certainly not critical.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Let's see; at about a 12 o'clock position ought to be a good sampling station.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, we're heading off now at (a heading of) 025.
Heading directly toward the southern rim of Dune.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: The mag's run out on your camera, Jim.
You should shut that off, and we don't want you to stray too far from your Rover tracks.
Head back more or less the way you came.
We have time for about a 10-minute stop someplace south and perhaps a little west of Dune Crater.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We're just on the rim of
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jimmy, did you turn the camera off?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I did, Joe, but apparently it didn't run past...I still have about 45 percent left.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I have turned it off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: This is a good spot right here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, look at those large blocks on that west wall.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Look at the large one right here.
Okay, let me get this (seatbelt) off.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Standing by for your mark when you stop.
And either Dave or Jim, we're going to need our (TV) camera and LCRU and the camera
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We're reading 292, (repeating the heading) 292, (bearing) 347, (distance driven) 8.9, (range) 3.4, 94, 100, 89, 90; motor temps, both low.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And if you think you can make this a 10-minute stop, we'll forego the TV.
We're interested in keeping it pretty short.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) And a rake might be useful...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...your choice on getting us a few samples.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: For a 10-minute stop, Dave, I don't think the rake is...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Just depending on however you read the fragment(-size) distribution.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: There are a lot of large fragments here, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, I've got to change my film Mag here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, I'll take the (sample documentation) pictures.
(Answering Joe) Jim's out of film, or his camera stopped, and I can take the pictures.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't we move it over here to a...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...did you change your mag out at the last stop?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Jim, let's get down here by these boulders.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think we can get them a pretty good distribution.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Joe, I have a partial pan there, and my camera stopped working.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, you're going to have to take the pictures.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I'll take all the pictures, if you'll get the...(Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, let me get another one (meaning another sample).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Give me the bag and you get some soil here.
I want to get that one, too.
(Pause as Jim puts some soil in the bag, undoubtedly with the scoop) Okay; good.
Why don't you zip the bag.
And let me get that other big rock.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What number was that, you remember?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, hold this bag, and it's number 174.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And there's one before that that came off Jim's bag rack (meaning Jim's pack of individual sample bags).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And was that an empty one?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Read the number on my bag (meaning the top bag in the holder.)
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Responding to Joe) Joe, just...We'll get to you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, back up a little bit, Jim, so I can get the ("after") picture.

tokens: Put that in my pack (meaning his SCB).
Let's go get a couple more.

tokens: The large gray one to your right with vesicles in it.

tokens: (Pause; approaching the large boulder) Huge vesicles.

Man, look at the laths, Jim; it's beautiful.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Dave, we're going to have to sack that one up and think about moving on.

tokens: You going to try and knock a piece off, here?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Okay, if you'll hold my tongs, here.

(Pause) Should be able to get it right here in the middle.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Chuckling about the size of the piece he's knocked off) (I'll) put this (hammer) back (on Jim's PLSS).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Now put that large one in my pack.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, shoot, you dropped your pack (during the drive from Spur)!
Fortunately, it wasn't the one with the rocks in it.

tokens: I don't know what it had in it, but it sure didn't have those good rocks in it; and that's why I put those good rocks in the...Oh, well; win a few and lose a few.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Put that in my pack (SCB); will you, Jim?
Okay; this is a large corner of a vesicular rock that's a big boulder sitting here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just about all we're going to be able to put in your bag.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Hey, maybe...Let me get those two frags there from the center.

tokens: You can get out a bag.

tokens: ...head them up and point them out.

tokens: "Head them up and point them out." (Bag) 204.
Oh, let me get the other one.
Two frags from the center of the...

tokens: 204 for the two frags in the center of the boulder.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And that's not much for Dune, but I think it's representative.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I hope it's representative because it...(Responding to Joe) Okay.
Put that in my bag, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Rover, you should be able to easily follow your tracks...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Boy, underneath that one is another one with larger vesicles in it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave and Jim, as you climb back on, we'd like an EMU status check from both of you, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, I'm reading pressure, 3.85 (psi); (warning) flags are clear, and 50 percent (oxygen remaining).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Jim, when you get settled, you can check the camera on 12 frames per second and turn it on again, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I don't know why it didn't come on last time, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, check it at 12 frames per second, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Just sit back as you can now.
Can you hold these things for me, Jim?
(Pause) (Thinking about the missing SCB) Oh, no; I didn't put a bag on you, did I?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What did you do with that bag?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I stuck it on the Hand Tool Carrier so it -
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, I had me worried, too.
I knew the one with the good rocks, I hadn't lost, because I stuck that in the seat pan.
But I thought I'd put one on you, and now I remember I started to put it on you, and your harness looked loose, so I stuck it on the handtool carrier where it's got a lock.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we knew all the time, Dave.
(Pause) Wanted to keep you honest, though.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, it looks like the camera is working, Joe.

tokens: We'll try it again on our drive back.

tokens: We'll be standing by for your mark.
